mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending and driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.0 over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales amplify aggregate demand signals, leading to stronger corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline by at least 0.5% in cumulative returns over any 12-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, rising industrial production indicates capacity utilization improvements that directly translate to higher profit margins and positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth rates during identified recovery episodes (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent signals robust economic momentum that sustains positive equity risk premiums and drives S&P 500 gains over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 for three consecutive years despite GDP growth consistently exceeding 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates crossing above 0.15 percent signal the onset of monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances despite the rate crossing the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth acceleration and unemployment decline confirm labor market normalization, reducing recession risk and supporting sustained equity outperformance over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day returns over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion environments, rapid retail sales growth precedes margin compression and earnings disappointments, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly negative when RSAFS growth exceeds the 75th percentile during late-cycle phases.
